zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Jane Beauchamp於大約1505年出生在英國時，她的父親Sir Anthony Beauchamp19歲，母親Lady Anne West27歲。她於1529年之前與Thomas Anderson在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1599年2月16日在英國貝德福爾享年95歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Jane Beauchamp于大约1505年出生在英国时，她的父亲Sir Anthony Beauchamp19岁，母亲Lady Anne West27岁。她于1529年之前与Thomas Anderson在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1599年2月16日在英国貝德福爾享年95岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Jane Beauchamp was born about 1505, in England, her father, Sir Anthony Beauchamp, was 19 and her mother, Lady Anne West, was 27. She married Thomas Anderson before 1529, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 16 February 1599, in Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 95.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Jane Beauchamp est née environ 1505, à Angleterre, son père, Sir Anthony Beauchamp, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Lady Anne West, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Anderson avant 1529, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 16 février 1599, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 95 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Jane Beauchamp um 1505 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Anthony Beauchamp 19 und ihre Mutter Lady Anne West 27. Sie hat vor 1529 in England, United Kingdom Thomas Anderson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 16. Februar 1599 im Alter von 95 Jahren in Bedfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Jane Beauchamp è nata il circa 1505 nel England, sua padre, Sir Anthony Beauchamp, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, Lady Anne West, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Anderson prima del 1529, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 16 febbraio 1599, nel Bedfordshire, England, all'età di 95 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Jane Beauchampが約1505年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Sir Anthony Beauchampは19歳，母Lady Anne Westは27歳であった。1529年以前にイギリスイギリスでThomas Andersonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1599年2月16日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアで95歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Jane Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1505년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Anthony Beauchamp(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Anne West(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1529년 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Thomas Anderson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1599년 2월 16일 영국 베드퍼드실르에서 95세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Jane Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1505, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Anthony Beauchamp, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Lady Anne West, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Anderson antes 1529, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 16 de fevereiro de 1599, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 95 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Jane Beauchamp родилась около 1505 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Anthony Beauchamp было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Anne West, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Anderson перед 1529 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 16 февраля 1599 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 95 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Jane Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1505, en Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Anthony Beauchamp, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Lady Anne West, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Thomas Anderson antes de 1529, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 16 de febrero de 1599, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 95 años.
